sm_120)\u003c/strong\u003e with \u003cstrong\u003eWSL2\u003c/strong\u003e (CUDA 12.8, PyTorch 2.8).\nTriton kernels are architecture-agnostic (no sm_120-specific code), so they should work on other NVIDIA GPUs (A100, H100, RTX 4090, etc.), but this has \u003cstrong\u003enot been verified\u003c/strong\u003e. If you test on a different GPU, please open an issue or PR with your results!\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003chr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOmniVoice-Triton replaces performance-critical operators in \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/k2-fsa/OmniVoice\"\u003eOmniVoice\u003c/a\u003e with hand-written \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/triton-lang/triton\"\u003eTriton\u003c/a\u003e kernels. Inspired by \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/linkedin/Liger-Kernel\"\u003eLiger Kernel\u003c/a\u003e (LinkedIn), each kernel fuses multiple HBM round-trips into a single pass, reducing memory traffic without any additional VRAM usage.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eOmniVoice uses a \u003cstrong\u003eNon-Autoregressive (NAR) iterative unmasking\u003c/strong\u003e architecture: 32 forward passes through the full 28-layer Qwen3-0.6B backbone generate the entire token sequence in parallel. Unlike autoregressive TTS where the sequence grows token by token, OmniVoice fixes the sequence length (determined by the duration predictor) and progressively refines all positions simultaneously. This makes it \u003cstrong\u003edeterministic with a fixed seed\u003c/strong\u003e — the same input always produces the same output.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis NAR structure creates two optimization opportunities absent in standard AR models:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003col dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCUDA Graph\u003c/strong\u003e: Since sequence length is fixed across all 32 unmasking steps, every forward pass has the \u003cstrong\u003esame tensor shape\u003c/strong\u003e. CUDA Graph captures the entire 28-layer forward pass once and replays it — eliminating ~900 kernel launch overheads per utterance.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTriton Kernel Fusion\u003c/strong\u003e: The Qwen3-0.6B backbone uses RMSNorm and SwiGLU activations. These are memory-bandwidth-bound on modern GPUs; fusing them reduces HBM round-trips from 4→1 (RMSNorm) and eliminates intermediate tensors (SwiGLU).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ol\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe \u003cstrong\u003eHybrid\u003c/strong\u003e mode — Triton kernels applied before CUDA Graph capture — achieves \u003cstrong\u003e3.4x end-to-end speedup\u003c/strong\u003e with only ~50MB additional VRAM.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e💡 Why Triton?\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content--why-triton\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 💡 Why Triton?\" href=\"#-why-triton\"\u003e\u003csvg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e🪶 \u003cstrong\u003eLightweight \u0026amp; Portable\u003c/strong\u003e — No serving infrastructure needed. Just \u003ccode\u003epip install omnivoice-triton\u003c/code\u003e and call \u003ccode\u003ecreate_runner(\"hybrid\")\u003c/code\u003e. Works in standalone scripts, Gradio apps, or any Python environment.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e🎯 \u003cstrong\u003eDeterministic NAR TTS\u003c/strong\u003e — OmniVoice is approximately deterministic at temperature=0. With Hybrid mode's \u003cstrong\u003e~3.4x speedup\u003c/strong\u003e, you can generate speech in under 170ms for typical utterances — making real-time interactive TTS practical.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e✨ <p dir="auto"><strong>Up to 3.4x faster OmniVoice inference through Triton kernel fusion and CUDA Graph optimization.</strong></p>
<p dir="auto"><a href="/newgrit1004/omnivoice-triton/blob/main/README_ko.md">Korean (한국어)</a> | <a href="/newgrit1004/omnivoice-triton/blob/main/docs/benchmark_results_en.md">Benchmark Results</a></p>
<div class="markdown-alert markdown-alert-note" dir="auto"><p class="markdown-alert-title" dir="auto"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-info mr-2" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="M0 8a8 8 0 1 1 16 0A8 8 0 0 1 0 8Zm8-6.5a6.5 6.5 0 1 0 0 13 6.5 6.5 0 0 0 0-13ZM6.5 7.75A.75.75 0 0 1 7.25 7h1a.75.75 0 0 1 .75.75v2.75h.25a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-2a.75.75 0 0 1 0-1.5h.25v-2h-.25a.75.75 0 0 1-.75-.75ZM8 6a1 1 0 1 1 0-2 1 1 0 0 1 0 2Z"></path></svg>Note</p><p dir="auto">This project has only been tested on <strong>RTX 5090 (Blackwell, sm_120)</strong> with <strong>WSL2</strong> (CUDA 12.8, PyTorch 2.8).
Triton kernels are architecture-agnostic (no sm_120-specific code), so they should work on other NVIDIA GPUs (A100, H100, RTX 4090, etc.), but this has <strong>not been verified</strong>. If you test on a different GPU, please open an issue or PR with your results!</p>
</div>
<hr>
<p dir="auto">OmniVoice-Triton replaces performance-critical operators in <a href="https://github.com/k2-fsa/OmniVoice">OmniVoice</a> with hand-written <a href="https://github.com/triton-lang/triton">Triton</a> kernels. Inspired by <a href="https://github.com/linkedin/Liger-Kernel">Liger Kernel</a> (LinkedIn), each kernel fuses multiple HBM round-trips into a single pass, reducing memory traffic without any additional VRAM usage.</p>
<p dir="auto">OmniVoice uses a <strong>Non-Autoregressive (NAR) iterative unmasking</strong> architecture: 32 forward passes through the full 28-layer Qwen3-0.6B backbone generate the entire token sequence in parallel. Unlike autoregressive TTS where the sequence grows token by token, OmniVoice fixes the sequence length (determined by the duration predictor) and progressively refines all positions simultaneously. This makes it <strong>deterministic with a fixed seed</strong> — the same input always produces the same output.</p>
<p dir="auto">This NAR structure creates two optimization opportunities absent in standard AR models:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li>
<p dir="auto"><strong>CUDA Graph</strong>: Since sequence length is fixed across all 32 unmasking steps, every forward pass has the <strong>same tensor shape</strong>. CUDA Graph captures the entire 28-layer forward pass once and replays it — eliminating ~900 kernel launch overheads per utterance.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Triton Kernel Fusion</strong>: The Qwen3-0.6B backbone uses RMSNorm and SwiGLU activations. These are memory-bandwidth-bound on modern GPUs; fusing them reduces HBM round-trips from 4→1 (RMSNorm) and eliminates intermediate tensors (SwiGLU).</p>
</li>
</ol>
<p dir="auto">The <strong>Hybrid</strong> mode — Triton kernels applied before CUDA Graph capture — achieves <strong>3.4x end-to-end speedup</strong> with only ~50MB additional VRAM.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">💡 Why Triton?</h3><a id="user-content--why-triton" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 💡 Why Triton?" href="#-why-triton"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>🪶 <strong>Lightweight & Portable</strong> — No serving infrastructure needed. Just <code>pip install omnivoice-triton</code> and call <code>create_runner("hybrid")</code>. Works in standalone scripts, Gradio apps, or any Python environment.</li>
<li>🎯 <strong>Deterministic NAR TTS</strong> — OmniVoice is approximately deterministic at temperature=0. With Hybrid mode's <strong>~3.4x speedup</strong>, you can generate speech in under 170ms for typical utterances — making real-time interactive TTS practical.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">✨ Highlights</h3><a id="user-content--highlights" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ✨ Highlights" href="#-highlights"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>⚡ <strong>3 Fused Triton Kernels</strong> — RMSNorm, SwiGLU, Fused Norm+Residual</li>
<li>🎯 <strong>6 Inference Modes</strong> — Base, Triton, Triton+Sage, Faster, Hybrid, Hybrid+Sage</li>
<li>🔬 <strong>3-Tier Verification</strong> — Kernel correctness → Model parity → E2E quality</li>
<li>💾 <strong>~50MB Extra VRAM</strong> — Pure kernel fusion, no model changes</li>
<li>🔌 <strong>Drop-in Patching</strong> — Single <code>create_runner()</code> call with mode selection</li>
<li>📊 <strong>Streamlit Dashboard</strong> — Side-by-side comparison UI with live metrics</li>
<li>🗣️ <strong>3 Voice Modes</strong> — Auto TTS, Voice Cloning, Voice Design</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">📦 Install</h2><a id="user-content--install" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 📦 Install" href="#-install"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Requirements</strong>: Python 3.12+, CUDA 12.8+, NVIDIA GPU (~2GB VRAM). Tested on WSL2 (Windows Subsystem for Linux 2).</p>

<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>UV handles virtual environments automatically</strong> — no need to manually activate a venv.
All commands use the <code>uv run</code> prefix (e.g., <code>uv run pytest</code>, <code>uv run python script.py</code>).
PyTorch is installed from the <a href="https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu128" rel="nofollow">cu128 index</a> automatically via <code>pyproject.toml</code>.</p>
</blockquote>

<div class="highlight highlight-source-python notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s1">omnivoice_triton</span> <span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">create_runner</span>
<span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">soundfile</span> <span class="pl-k">as</span> <span class="pl-s1">sf</span>

<span class="pl-s1">runner</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-en">create_runner</span>(<span class="pl-s">"triton"</span>)
<span class="pl-s1">runner</span>.<span class="pl-c1">load_model</span>()

<span class="pl-s1">result</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">runner</span>.<span class="pl-c1">generate</span>(
    <span class="pl-s1">text</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"Hello, this is optimized with Triton kernels."</span>,
)

<span class="pl-c"># Save audio</span>
<span class="pl-s1">sf</span>.<span class="pl-c1">write</span>(<span class="pl-s">"output.wav"</span>, <span class="pl-s1">result</span>[<span class="pl-s">"audio"</span>], <span class="pl-s1">result</span>[<span class="pl-s">"sample_rate"</span>])
<span class="pl-en">print</span>(<span class="pl-s">f"Generated in <span class="pl-s1"><span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-s1">result</span>[<span class="pl-s">'time_ms'</span>]:.1f<span class="pl-kos">}</span></span>ms, VRAM: <span class="pl-s1"><span class="pl-kos">{</span><span class="pl-s1">result</span>[<span class="pl-s">'peak_vram_gb'</span>]:.2f<span class="pl-kos">}</span></span>GB"</span>)

<span class="pl-s1">runner</span>.<span class="pl-c1">unload_model</span>()</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="from omnivoice_triton import create_runner
import soundfile as sf

runner = create_runner("triton")
runner.load_model()

result = runner.generate(
    text="Hello, this is optimized with Triton kernels.",
)

# Save audio
sf.write("output.wav", result["audio"], result["sample_rate"])
print(f"Generated in {result['time_ms']:.1f}ms, VRAM: {result['peak_vram_gb']:.2f}GB")

runner.unload_model()" tabindex="0" role="button">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">⚡ Triton Kernels</h2><a id="user-content--triton-kernels" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: ⚡ Triton Kernels" href="#-triton-kernels"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">All kernels target the <strong>OmniVoice LLM backbone</strong> (Qwen3-0.6B, 28-layer Transformer, hidden_size=1024, intermediate=3072).</p>

<thead>
<tr>
<th>Kernel</th>
<th>What It Fuses</th>
<th>HBM Savings</th>
<th>File</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>RMSNorm</strong></td>
<td>variance + normalize + scale in SRAM</td>
<td>4→1 round-trips</td>
<td><code>kernels/rms_norm.py</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>SwiGLU</strong></td>
<td><code>silu(gate) * up</code> — eliminates intermediate tensor</td>
<td>3→1 round-trips</td>
<td><code>kernels/swiglu.py</code></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Fused Norm+Residual</strong></td>
<td><code>residual + x</code> then RMSNorm in one kernel</td>
<td>2 kernels → 1</td>
<td><code>kernels/fused_norm_residual.py</code></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🔌 How Patching Works</h3><a id="user-content--how-patching-works" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🔌 How Patching Works" href="#-how-patching-works"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><code>apply_triton_kernels()</code> performs in-place monkey-patching:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li><strong>RMSNorm modules</strong> → replaced with <code>TritonRMSNorm</code> (shares original weights, zero copy)</li>
<li><strong>MLP forward</strong> → patched to use <code>triton_swiglu_forward</code> (fused gate+up projection)</li>
<li><strong>Decoder layer forward</strong> → patched for fused residual addition + normalization</li>
</ol>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-python notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s1">omnivoice_triton</span>.<span class="pl-s1">models</span>.<span class="pl-s1">patching</span> <span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">apply_triton_kernels</span>

<span class="pl-c"># Patches all 28 decoder layers in-place (patch counts logged via logging)</span>
<span class="pl-en">apply_triton_kernels</span>(<span class="pl-s1">model</span>)</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="from omnivoice_triton.models.patching import apply_triton_kernels

# Patches all 28 decoder layers in-place (patch counts logged via logging)
apply_triton_kernels(model)" tabindex="0" role="button">

<summary><b>Advanced: Manual Patching</b></summary>
<p dir="auto">If you want to apply Triton kernels to a model loaded outside the Runner API:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-python notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s1">omnivoice</span> <span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-v">OmniVoice</span>
<span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s1">omnivoice_triton</span>.<span class="pl-s1">models</span>.<span class="pl-s1">patching</span> <span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">apply_triton_kernels</span>
<span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">torch</span>

<span class="pl-s1">model</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-v">OmniVoice</span>.<span class="pl-c1">from_pretrained</span>(
    <span class="pl-s">"k2-fsa/OmniVoice"</span>,
    <span class="pl-s1">device_map</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"cuda:0"</span>,
    <span class="pl-s1">dtype</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s1">torch</span>.<span class="pl-c1">bfloat16</span>,
)

<span class="pl-c"># Patch the internal LLM backbone</span>
<span class="pl-en">apply_triton_kernels</span>(<span class="pl-s1">model</span>.<span class="pl-c1">llm</span>)</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="from omnivoice import OmniVoice
from omnivoice_triton.models.patching import apply_triton_kernels

model = OmniVoice.from_pretrained(
    "k2-fsa/OmniVoice",
    device_map="cuda:0",
    dtype=torch.bfloat16,
)

# Patch the internal LLM backbone
apply_triton_kernels(model.llm)" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">For Hybrid mode with manual patching, Triton kernels must be applied before CUDA Graph capture:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-python notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">from</span> <span class="pl-s1">omnivoice_triton</span>.<span class="pl-s1">models</span>.<span class="pl-s1">patching</span> <span class="pl-k">import</span> <span class="pl-s1">apply_triton_kernels</span>

<span class="pl-c"># Apply Triton patches first</span>
<span class="pl-en">apply_triton_kernels</span>(<span class="pl-s1">model</span>.<span class="pl-c1">llm</span>)

<span class="pl-c"># Then capture CUDA Graph</span>
<span class="pl-s1">model</span>.<span class="pl-c1">capture_cuda_graph</span>()</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<p dir="auto">Hybrid mode generates audio at roughly 17x real-time on average — a 3.26x improvement over the PyTorch baseline.</p>
<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Disclaimer</strong>: Benchmarks measured on a single RTX 5090. Results vary with GPU model, driver version, system load, and input text length. Run <code>make bench</code> on your hardware for accurate numbers.</p>
</blockquote>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">🧠 OmniVoice Architecture</h3><a id="user-content--omnivoice-architecture" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: 🧠 OmniVoice Architecture" href="#-omnivoice-architecture"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">OmniVoice uses a Qwen3-0.6B LLM backbone for NAR iterative unmasking (32 steps, 8 codebooks × 1025 vocabulary).</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Parameter</th>
<th>Value</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Model</td>
<td>Qwen3-0.6B</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Hidden Size</td>
<td>1024</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Attention Heads</td>
<td>16 (GQA, kv_heads=8)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Head Dim</td>
<td>128</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Intermediate Size</td>
<td>3072</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Layers</td>
<td>28</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>RMS Norm Eps</td>
<td>1e-6</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Position Encoding</td>
<td>Standard RoPE</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Activation</td>
<td>SwiGLU</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Generation</td>
<td>NAR iterative unmasking (32 steps)</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>

<p dir="auto">OmniVoice supports three generation modes, all available across all runners:</p>

<thead>
<tr>
<th>Feature</th>
<th align="center">Base</th>
<th align="center">Triton</th>
<th align="center">Faster</th>
<th align="center">Hybrid</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Auto TTS</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Voice Cloning</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Voice Design</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Dynamic Shape</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>bfloat16 / float16</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
<td align="center">Yes</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Auto TTS</strong>: Standard text-to-speech with no reference audio.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Voice Cloning</strong>: Provide a reference audio file and its transcript to clone a speaker's identity.</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-python notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-s1">result</span> <span class="pl-c1">=</span> <span class="pl-s1">runner</span>.<span class="pl-c1">generate_voice_clone</span>(
    <span class="pl-s1">text</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"This is a cloned voice."</span>,
    <span class="pl-s1">ref_audio</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"reference.wav"</span>,
    <span class="pl-s1">ref_text</span><span class="pl-c1">=</span><span class="pl-s">"Transcript of the reference audio."</span>,
)</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
